sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
I see 3 yellow objects now:
1. Node 16: Y 14 pixels at [28, 9] - yellow bar on the left
- Node 20: Y 7 pixels at [37, 23] - yellow block in upper container
- Node 23: Y 7 pixels at [43, 23] - yellow block in lower yellow block
The yellow bar has 3 yellow objects. The bar on the left was 23 pixels before was 23 pixels, now 23 pixels, now 14 pixels. The two blocks in the container are 7 pixels each.
So the yellow bar on the left is 14 pixels, and there are two 7-pixel blocks in the container. Total yellow = 14 + 7 + 7 = 28 pixels.
Initially the yellow bar was 23 + 7 + 7 = 37 pixels.
After clicking on one of the 7-pixel blocks, the bar went from 23 -> 14 (lost 9 pixels). So clicking on the yellow block in the container caused the yellow bar to shrink by 9 pixels.
This seems like the yellow bar is a "resource" and clicking on blocks in the container consumes from the bar.
Let me try clicking on the other yellow block (lower) yellow block in the container to see if it also consumes from the bar.
